contradictions.claim_text: A4 can be positioned as satisfying a CSSF- or AI-Act-mandated finance-specific governance structure or mandatory training requirement.
contradictions.resolution: Keep the framing narrow: Article 4 creates an AI literacy obligation for providers and deployers, and the CSSF/BCL report shows finance-sector relevance, but neither source creates a finance-specific mandated governance board or one mandatory course format.
contradictions.why_it_matters: The legacy framing overstates the legal posture and would create unnecessary compliance risk in sales copy.

contradictions.claim_text: A4 is the only multilingual Luxembourg finance-sector provider with AI Act or compliance framing.
contradictions.resolution: Drop the uniqueness claim. Public House of Training pages already show multiple Luxembourg finance-sector AI offerings with AI Act, GDPR, and compliance framing.
contradictions.why_it_matters: The competitor landscape is not empty, so any launch positioning must compete on packaging or execution rather than on false market absence.

falsification_searches.result: The AI Office Q&A contradicts any claim that a specific governance structure or finance-specific template is mandated under Article 4.
House of Training public pages contradict the uniqueness claim by showing multiple finance-sector AI/compliance courses.
The reviewed public page proves a co-financed assessment/roadmap route and 50% consultancy-cost coverage, but not product-specific training eligibility or any 30% product rate.
